daily_reports.source_file: Manual entry via UI (2026-07-18) - No DRC file found

organized_notes.text: exit CSCO short @ 109 - stop hit, stock broke above a prior relative high

do not feel bad about UNH gapping up 10% on earnings - if you stayed in, it would only have reinforced bad behaviours. It is important to remember that earnings are binary events and that we only trade if we have an edge #flag
do not judge a decision by its outcome #flag

SPY bouncing off D1 8 EMA here - as Pete said, it's important not to read too much into the early consecutive red candles as we are in a tight range - we should expect two-sided action as neither side has conviction
space stocks are really weak and breaking major D1 support - RKLB ASTS PL #sector

Hari - There is a psychological dissonance that occurs - on the one hand everything between major technicals is "noise" (i.e. if it is still above X line then it remains a good trade) - on the other hand that "noise" is much of what we trade day-to-day.  So in one sense it is unimportant and on the other it is exactly what we trade.

regarding Hari's comment, I think it is very important to realise that the intraday noise we ignore when swing trading is what we trade when we day trade - the difference between 'reading price action' and 'ignoring noise' -  all depends on your timeframe
this is why it is so critical to be clear of your timeframe to avoid reading too much into noise (using the M5 for swing trades) #flag3
SPY nice bounce and back at HOD but I don't have enough confidence to go long - there's a good chance SPY just reverses back into the range
LCID nice long here
GOOGL breaking back above 50 SMA, could be nice if it rallies past HOD
AAPL looks good even on the D1 but sharp pullback on the M5 all the way to VWAP-
if AAPL can close above HOD on this candle i will go long
USAR really nice short setup - but market is bouncing rn
CSCO long looked really good for a swing on 10/7 what happened??? sector weakness? #flag #sector

Khorn - I’m down again on my tech longs, thoughts start creeping in like, “I can oversize this day trade to erase my losses today”. This is how accounts get blown, you deal with a drawdown, average down, over leverage, try to trade out of it on emotion. I’m experienced enough now to know what not to do. You can have a great few months and erase it plus more in days if you let a drawdown mess with your mental. Keep this in mind when dealing with drawdowns. This is what makes great traders, not the wins but managing the losses.
this is a really important point on the mental aspect of trading and a form of revenge trading - could be a completely valid setup but oversized due to urge to 'make back the loss' #flag2
CSCO nice short here
short CSCO @ 107.76, 3 shares - CSCO breaking LOD on heavy volume, market struggling to bounce off this compression low - targetting ATRL with a stop just above VWAP
Pete - AMAT needs to close above yesterday's open to be considered long
urgency is an important component when reading price action other than where the D1 candle closes #flag2

Pete - long green and red candles are landmarks. If they are instantly erased, that is a sign of aggressive buying or selling in the opposite direction. Ideally, they are consistent with the trend and we are trading a continuation and not a reversal #gold
![[Pasted image 20260716233224.png]]
getting ready to stop out on CSCO if this green candle is not erased - market is bouncing and CSCO is losing RS
it is important to notice that it is still LPTE right now - even as SPY looks nice it will make a new high, it has not made one yet and has PDH overhead resistance - do not load up on longs!!! #flag2
![[Pasted image 20260716233739.png]]
long AAPL @ 331.40, 4 shares - AAPL has clear RS and is breaking above HOD - note SPY is still not through resistance yet so we are still trading in LPTE - this was a FOMO entry #flag
zoom out and realise that we are stuck in a range and this rejection off the HOD was likely
this is also where we can see how my RS long - AAPL is giving me an edge over trading

![[Pasted image 20260716235441.png]] ![[Pasted image 20260716235501.png]]![[Pasted image 20260716235518.png]]![[Pasted image 20260716235530.png]]![[Pasted image 20260716235544.png]]![[Pasted image 20260716235608.png]]![[Pasted image 20260716235705.png]]![[Pasted image 20260716235713.png]]

in choppy conditions, trades often move instantly against you - that's why it's important to size down and give trades a chance to move around - you need staying power in choppy conditions whereas in HPTE, stops can be tight which is what gives us aggressive trades with larger size #flag #gold
exit AAPL long @ 331.46 - chased it on a dip so got a bad fill - exit on SPY weakness while AAPL held its gains, but should have stayed patient when AAPL pulled back
orders set for CSCO short

organized_notes.text: keep TRV long on the watchlist next week
watching MRK CSCO long
pharma sector price action looking really nice - watching MRK JNJ long closely #sector
CSCO looks really good here -

long CSCO @ 112.10, 4 shares - beautiful wimpy pullback continuing higher breaking HOD, supported by SPY on a nice strong gap reversal - just note that CSCO is not strong on the D1 and is below 8/15 EMA [TRADE: long CSCO @ 112.1 x4]
PANW really strong, FTNT nice long above PDH
watching GE long for a swing above 8 EMA - consider for a swing tmr if it closes above 8/15
SPY has a much stronger trend today than yesterday so i can expect more follow thru for my trades
XLF strong today - BAC JPM long
was bidding for JPM but got greedy and missed the fill before it popped - be patient and DO NOT CHASE - you will have a chance if it pulls back to enter at a better price or with more confirmation
long JPM @ 344.97, 1 share - patience paid off - got in with more confirmation as JPM pulled back and found support before moving higher [TRADE: long JPM @ 344.97 x1]
watch SPY - starting to transition sideways
CSCO showing signs of resistance with many upper wicks
MRK looks really nice for a long but i am hesitating because of the low volume and SPY pulling back
review CSCO vs JPM - why did CSCO pullback while JPM continued higher? #flag
TRV strong
SPY trying to bounce off the 50 SMA
![[Pasted image 20260717233036.png]]

Dan - it's very rare that momentum in relation to the 50 sma re accelerates without that. You can see how all the easy swing money happens fast out of those dips below the 50, one the peak happens and lower highs starts establishing, the buying strength gets weaker and weaker until some kind of event or a ranging/topping then a reset. on the downslopes from peak, price still creeps up, but gets choppier with more dips. You then get a ~2adr or a ~4 adr or a ~5-6+ adr (big market scare) flush below the 50. the ~4 ish adr ones are the ones that most likely set up a nice low adr grindy bull trend again. pic is ~5 years of d1 #flag2
so far SPY looks good - it was crucial that we bounced off here and the long greens held
exit orders set for CSCO

got in with more confirmation as JPM pulled back and found support before moving higher [TRADE: long JPM @ 344.97 x1]
